title: Project Obsidian
subtitle: Iceland and the Faroe Islands on the Norwegian Star

team.note: Five of us: Mom, Dad, Kilian, and Grandma and Grandpa in the room down the hall. Obsidian is black volcanic glass, made the real way exactly like Kilian makes it in Minecraft: lava meets water. Iceland is the biggest obsidian farm on Earth.

bring: Waterproof hiking boots, broken in. Non-negotiable for the sea cliffs and the lava fields
Base layer: wool or synthetic, no cotton
Mid layer: heavy fleece or wool
Outer shell: waterproof AND windproof. Umbrellas are useless here
Warm hat and gloves even in August. It is 50 to 55F with hard wind
Passports for everyone, plus the cruise boarding passes
Seasick meds for the Aug 15 crossing of the Iceland-Faroe Ridge
Swimsuits and flip flops. Sky Lagoon on day one, and Iceland makes you shower properly first
Portable battery and a plug adapter (Europlug Type F, 230V)
Eye mask for Kilian. Mid-August Reykjavik never really gets dark

bookings.title: Flight out: Icelandair 852, ORD to KEF
bookings.note: Overnight crossing, lands 6:40am Saturday. Miss this one and the airline cancels the flight home too, so do not cut it fine.

bookings.title: Old Town Reykjavik
bookings.note: One night before the ship. Walkable to everything downtown.

bookings.title: Norwegian Star, 7 nights
bookings.note: Freestyle cruising. No fixed dining times, no formal nights.

bookings.title: Flight home: Icelandair 855, KEF to ORD
bookings.note: Lands O'Hare 9:25pm the same evening. Flying west you gain 5 hours back.

bookings.title: Online check-in with Norwegian
bookings.note: Opens Jul 19, 21 days out. Needs passport numbers, an emergency contact who is not sailing, and a card for the onboard account.

bookings.title: Sky Lagoon Pure Pass Adventure, with transfer
bookings.note: 3 hours. Hotel checkout is also 11:00, so bags go down before the desk shuts at 11. Ask where the transfer drops you.

bookings.title: Snaefellsnes Highlights and Kirkjufell Views by bus
bookings.note: 4 hours. Tender port, so get tender tickets the moment you dock at 7.

bookings.title: Half Day Lake Myvatn and Godafoss Waterfall
bookings.note: 5.5 hours, the longest excursion of the week.

bookings.title: Puffins and Wildlife Cruising
bookings.note: 2.25 hours by boat. An NCL tour, so the ship waits for it. Back by 12:30 against a hard 2pm all-aboard.

bookings.title: Vestmanna Seacliffs
bookings.note: 4 hours. Ship docks 11:30 and this leaves at 12:00 - be at the gangway before you dock.

bookings.title: Seydisfjordur: walk the town
bookings.note: Nothing to book. Rainbow Street, the Tvisongur sound sculpture, and a waterfall loop if the weather holds.

bookings.title: Blue Lagoon on the last day
bookings.note: Nothing is booked for Aug 16 and there are eleven hours to fill. Sells out - book early.

bookings.title: Flybus: cruise terminal to Blue Lagoon to airport
bookings.note: About $29 to $35 a head instead of the ship's $99.50.

bookings.title: ORD parking, Aug 7 to 16
bookings.note: Ten days, Aug 7 to 16. Icelandair is Terminal 5 both ways, which rules out PreFlight - its return shuttle only serves Terminal 2. Not confirmed paid.

lists.title: The whole point
lists.note: The reasons we flew across an ocean. Do not come home without these.

items.text: Find real obsidian. Actual volcanic glass, in the wild

items: Puffins from the boat at Isafjordur, before they leave for the winter
Kirkjufell, the most photographed mountain in Iceland
Godafoss, the waterfall of the gods
Stand in the Faroe Islands, a place most people never get to

lists.title: Up for grabs
lists.note: Nothing is booked for these. The Seydisfjordur day is completely open.
items: The rainbow street and the blue church at Seydisfjordur
Blue Lagoon on the way to the airport on the last day
Spot the heart-shaped traffic lights in Akureyri
Tinganes old town in Torshavn, red turf roofs on the harbour
Be on deck for the sail-in to Seydisfjordur

notes: Tipping is not expected in Iceland or the Faroe Islands. Service is already in the price.
The Faroes run one hour ahead of Iceland in summer. The ship stays on Iceland time. Do not trust your phone in Torshavn.
Iceland is card-first. You can go the whole trip without touching cash.
Grundarfjordur is a tender port. Tender tickets are the first thing you do, not the thing you get around to.
Isafjordur all-aboard at 2pm is hard, but the puffin cruise is an NCL tour, so the ship waits for that one.
Grandma and Grandpa fly separately and stay on after we go home. Their days are not our days.

facts: Iceland is one of the only countries on Earth with zero mosquitoes. Nobody is totally sure why.
The Icelandic word for obsidian is hrafntinna, which means raven's flint.
There is a mountain in Iceland called Hrafntinnusker, or Obsidian Skerry, with shiny black volcanic glass all over the ground.
Real obsidian can be chipped into a blade sharper than a steel knife, so ancient people used it for tools.
Almost all of Iceland's heat and power comes from volcanoes and water, not from burning fuel.
There are more sheep in Iceland than there are people.
Many Icelanders tell stories of the huldufolk, the hidden people, and some roads were even curved around elf rocks.
Iceland's parliament, the Althing, first met in the year 930, making it one of the oldest in the world.
Puffins spend all summer on the cliffs, then fly out to the open ocean in the middle of August.
A waterfall called Svartifoss is framed by stone columns that look like the pipes of a giant organ.
Reynisfjara is a beach in Iceland where the sand is black, made from ground up lava.
In August the sun barely sets in Iceland and it never gets fully dark.

trivia.q: In Minecraft you make obsidian when water touches lava. In real life, what makes obsidian?
choices: Lava that cools super fast, usually in water or cold air
Water that freezes into black ice
Sand squished for a million years
trivia.fact: It cools too fast to grow crystals, so it freezes into shiny black glass. Iceland, where hot lava meets the cold sea, is one of the best places on Earth for it.

trivia.q: What is the Icelandic word for obsidian?
trivia.fact: Hrafntinna means raven's flint. There is even a mountain named Hrafntinnusker, Obsidian Skerry, with black glass all over the ground.

trivia.q: Basalt columns in Iceland come out shaped like what?
choices: Six sided pillars, like stacked blocks
trivia.fact: When thick lava cools slowly and evenly it cracks into neat hexagons. Nature builds its own Minecraft blocks.

trivia.q: Iceland sits on a giant crack where two of Earth's plates pull apart. Which two?
trivia.fact: You can stand with one foot on each plate. Iceland is pulling apart about as fast as your fingernails grow.

trivia.q: How does a lava cave, or lava tube, form?
choices: Lava drains out and leaves an empty stone tunnel
Ice melts a hole in a mountain
trivia.fact: The top and sides of a lava river harden into rock, then the last lava drains out the bottom, leaving a hollow pipe you can walk through. Vatnshellir is about 8,000 years old.

trivia.q: Which animal does Iceland famously have NONE of?
trivia.fact: Iceland is one of the only countries on Earth with zero mosquitoes.

trivia.q: Icelandic horses can do a special smooth extra way of running. What is it called?
trivia.fact: Wild rule: if an Icelandic horse ever leaves the country it is never allowed back, and no outside horses are let in. It has been that way about 1,000 years.

trivia.q: Instead of a family last name, most Icelanders' names end in...
trivia.fact: Kilian would be something like Kilian Michaelsson, meaning Michael's son. The phone book is even sorted by first name.

trivia.q: In Minecraft, what is the weakest pickaxe that can actually mine obsidian?
trivia.fact: Diamond or netherite only. Obsidian has the highest blast resistance in the game, so even TNT and creepers cannot break it.

trivia.q: In August, how much daylight does Iceland get each day?
choices: Zero, it is dark all month
trivia.fact: The sun barely sets and it never gets fully dark. That is why an eye mask is on the packing list.

closingNote: Salt in the air, obsidian in the backpack, everybody home safe.

places.name: Chicago O'Hare
places.desc: International departures. Bag drop closes an hour before the flight.

places.name: Keflavik Airport
places.desc: 45 minutes from Reykjavik, 20 from the Blue Lagoon. The Flybus runs both legs.

places.name: Blue Lagoon
places.desc: Between the city and the airport, and it stores your luggage. The answer to the 11-hour gap on the last day. Book ahead, it sells out.

places.name: Old Town Reykjavik
places.desc: One night before the ship, right on the old town square.

places.name: Sky Lagoon
places.desc: Geothermal infinity edge over the Atlantic, on the Reykjavik side. The seven-step Skjol ritual is the whole point.

places.name: Skarfabakki Pier 312
places.desc: The cruise pier. 10 to 12 minutes from downtown by taxi, about 4,000 ISK.

places.name: Grundarfjordur
places.desc: Tender port. The village is 500m from the landing.

places.name: Kirkjufell
places.desc: The arrowhead mountain, 3km from the pier. The waterfall is on the near side.

places.name: Vatnshellir Lava Cave
places.desc: 8,000-year-old lava tubes on Snaefellsnes. Ages 5 and up.

places.name: Akureyri, Oddeyrarbryggja
places.desc: Capital of the north. The ship docks right at the town.

places.name: Lake Myvatn
places.desc: Volcanic lake, pseudocraters and steaming ground. The Diamond Circle, an hour east of Akureyri.

places.name: Godafoss
places.desc: The waterfall of the gods, 45 minutes east of Akureyri.

places.name: Forest Lagoon
places.desc: Infinity pools in a birch forest above the fjord.

places.name: Isafjordur, Sundabakki
places.desc: Westfjords hub. Walk-off pier. Hard 2pm all-aboard.

places.name: Vigur Island
places.desc: 100,000 puffins, plus eiders and arctic terns. They start leaving mid-August.

places.name: Dynjandi Waterfall
places.desc: Tiered cliff waterfall. Getting there means mountain tunnels that can delay you.

places.name: Seydisfjordur
places.desc: The rainbow street and the blue church, at the head of a narrow eastern fjord.

places.name: Torshavn, Faroe Islands
places.desc: The smallest capital in the world. Local time runs one hour ahead of the ship.

places.name: Vestmanna Sea Cliffs
places.desc: Basalt grottos full of nesting seabirds. A 45 minute drive before the boat even starts.

places.name: Kirkjubour
places.desc: Medieval village walk, 3 to 4 hours. The red bus back to the pier is free.

places.name: Tinganes Old Town
places.desc: Red turf-roofed government houses on the harbour spit. Easy walk from the ship.

places.name: Tvisongur Sound Sculpture
places.desc: Five concrete domes on the mountainside. Each one resonates a different note. Stand inside and sing and it sings back. 20 minutes uphill, free, always open.

places.name: Vestdalsfossar trail
places.desc: Family-friendly waterfall chain up the Vestdalur valley. Short loop is 5.5km, about 90 minutes.

places.name: Skaftfell Bistro and Art Center
places.desc: Lunch and an exhibition in town. Indoors, which matters when the alternative is horizontal rain.

days.title: Fly out
notes: Kilian skips the last day of camp, so this day has room in it now. Keep the room.

schedule.title: Drive to O'Hare
schedule.note: Leaving early is the whole plan. Friday evening into Chicago is the part that used to break.

schedule.title: Park and shuttle to the terminal
schedule.note: Off-site lots add 20 to 30 minutes. Terminal 5 for Icelandair.

schedule.title: Bag drop, security, gate
schedule.note: International bag drop closes 60 minutes out.

schedule.title: Icelandair 852 departs
schedule.note: 7:25pm, not 7:45. The ticket says so. Overnight, 6h15, lands Reykjavik 6:40am.

days.title: Land in Reykjavik
notes: You lose a night to the flight. Go easy and stay up until a normal bedtime.

schedule.title: Transfer into Reykjavik
schedule.note: 45 minutes. Flybus or a pre-booked van.

schedule.title: Drop bags, walk the old town
schedule.note: Rooms will not be ready this early. Leave the bags and go.

schedule.title: Reykjavik at your own pace
schedule.note: Hallgrimskirkja, the harbour, Braud and Co. Nothing scheduled on purpose.

days.title: Sky Lagoon, then board
notes: Checkout is 11:00 and the Sky Lagoon starts at 11:00. The bags have to be down before the front desk shuts at 11.
All aboard is 17:00, not 19:00. The ship does not wait.

schedule.title: Pack, bags to the front desk
schedule.note: Desk is staffed 7 to 11 only. Do not leave this to 10:55.

schedule.title: Check out
schedule.note: Checkout is 11:00 sharp and the excursion starts at 11:00. This is the tight seam of the day.

schedule.title: Sky Lagoon Pure Pass Adventure
schedule.note: 3 hours. Infinity edge over the Atlantic and the seven-step ritual. Transfer is included - confirm where it drops you.

schedule.title: To Skarfabakki Pier 312
schedule.note: With the luggage. If the Sky Lagoon transfer does not do this leg, it is a taxi and you want a minivan.

schedule.title: Embarkation
schedule.note: Sign Kilian up for Splash Academy today, not later in the week.

schedule.title: All aboard, then sailaway
schedule.note: Ship departs 7pm. Watch Reykjavik go by from the top deck.

days.title: Grundarfjordur
notes: Tender port. Tickets first, everything else second.

schedule.title: Arrive, get tender tickets
schedule.note: First thing. Late tickets cost you shore hours.

schedule.title: Snaefellsnes Highlights and Kirkjufell
schedule.note: 4 hours by bus. The arrowhead mountain everyone photographs.

schedule.title: Last tender
schedule.note: Ship leaves at 4pm. Do not cut this fine.

days.title: Akureyri and the Diamond Circle
notes: The longest day in port and the best one for whales.

schedule.title: Dock at Oddeyrarbryggja
schedule.note: Walk off, straight into town.

schedule.title: Lake Myvatn and Godafoss
schedule.note: 5.5 hours, the big one. Volcanic lake, pseudocraters, and the waterfall of the gods.

schedule.title: Akureyri town
schedule.note: Find the heart-shaped traffic lights.

schedule.title: Dinner at La Cocina
schedule.note: Table for 6 at 5:30.

days.title: Puffins at Isafjordur
notes: Seven hours and a hard 14:00 all-aboard. The puffin cruise is an NCL tour, so the ship waits for it.

schedule.title: Dock at Sundabakki
schedule.note: Walk-off pier, right at the town.

schedule.title: Isafjordur old town
schedule.note: Stay near the pier. The tour is the fixed point of the day.

schedule.title: Puffins and Wildlife Cruising
schedule.note: 2.25 hours by boat. Bring binoculars and the windproof layer - it is colder on the water.

schedule.title: All aboard. Hard stop
schedule.note: 90 minutes of margin after the tour. Do not spend it wandering.

days.title: Seydisfjordur on foot
notes: The only day with nothing booked, and the only day we get to pick. Nothing here needs a ticket.
The ship is a ten minute walk away all day. If the weather turns, just go back.

schedule.title: Sail in through the fjord
schedule.note: Narrow eastern fjord, mountains both sides. The prettiest arrival of the week. Worth an alarm.

schedule.title: Rainbow Street and the Blue Church
schedule.note: Five minutes off the pier. The painted street points straight at the church. Take the photo.

schedule.title: Up to Tvisongur
schedule.note: 20 minutes up a gravel road that starts across from the fish factory. Let Kilian yell in the domes - that is the whole point of them.

schedule.title: Vestdalsfossar loop, if the sky allows
schedule.note: 90 minute waterfall loop. If it is blowing, the Technology Museum in town is open 11 to 6 and is five minutes from the ship.

schedule.title: Back aboard
schedule.note: Ship departs 16:00, so all aboard is 15:30.

days.title: Torshavn, Faroe Islands
notes: Faroe local time is one hour ahead of the ship. Your phone will lie to you. Trust ship time.

schedule.title: Dock in Torshavn
schedule.note: SHIP time. Local clocks read an hour later. The tour leaves in 30 minutes - be at the gangway already.

schedule.title: Vestmanna Seacliffs
schedule.note: 4 hours. 45 minute drive, then the boat into the grottos.

schedule.title: Tinganes old town
schedule.note: Red turf-roofed houses on the harbour. Easy walk from the ship.

schedule.title: Dinner at Moderno
schedule.note: Table for 4 at 5:30.

schedule.title: All aboard
schedule.note: Ship time. Check your watch against the ship, not your phone.

days.title: At sea
notes: Crossing the Iceland-Faroe Ridge. Sea state here is genuinely variable.

schedule.title: Massage
schedule.note: Booked for 9:30.

schedule.title: Sea day. Do nothing
schedule.note: Splash Academy, the pool. Take the meds early if the swell builds - this is the Iceland-Faroe Ridge.

days.title: Blue Lagoon, then home
days.closingNote: Salt in the air, obsidian in the backpack, everybody home safe.
notes: You walk off at 7am and the plane leaves at 7:45pm. That is eleven hours. Do not spend them in an airport.
The Blue Lagoon sits between Reykjavik and Keflavik and stores luggage. That is the whole answer to this day.

schedule.title: Dock at Skarfabakki, walk off
schedule.note: Walk-off. Carry your own bags and you leave on your own schedule, not the ship's.

schedule.title: Transfer toward the Blue Lagoon
schedule.note: Flybus runs the cruise terminal to Blue Lagoon to airport route. Do not buy the ship's transfer for this.

schedule.title: Store the luggage
schedule.note: Roughly 550 ISK a bag, about $4. Lockers inside for the small stuff.

schedule.title: The Blue Lagoon
schedule.note: Book ahead. It sells out, and turning up without a slot means you do not get in.

schedule.title: Blue Lagoon to Keflavik
schedule.note: About 20 minutes. The Flybus does this leg on a schedule.

schedule.title: Check in, eat, fly
schedule.note: Icelandair 855 departs 7:45pm Iceland time and lands O'Hare 9:25pm the SAME evening. You gain 5 hours going west.

prep.title: Check all three names against the passports
prep.note: The tickets must match the passport exactly, and they cannot be changed - a mismatch is a new ticket, not a correction. Stacey asked this in February and nobody answered. Five minutes with three passports.

prep.title: Confirm we are actually on the booked excursions
prep.note: There are two shore excursion booking numbers and no statement of which reservation each covers. Check our three names appear. Same login as check-in.

prep.title: Ask NCL where the Sky Lagoon transfer drops us
prep.note: Aug 9: hotel checkout is 11:00 and the excursion starts at 11:00, and the front desk shuts at 11:00. Does the transfer go to the ship, and does it take luggage? Mom is already calling - make sure she asks the luggage half.

prep.title: Fix the flight times on the shared calendar
prep.note: The outbound says 7:45pm and the ticket says 7:25pm. The return is entered as a one-hour block in the wrong timezone with a Reykjavik address - it should read lands O'Hare 9:25pm on the 16th.

prep.title: Download the Kastali app
prep.note: The Reykjavik hotel front desk only exists 07:00-11:00. The app is how you get in outside that window, and we land at 06:40.

prep.title: Silence the recurring calendar events for the week
prep.note: Recovery grounding, Movement, Deep Work, the NA meeting on the 9th, Kilian shower time and Rooster roster all fire while we are in the North Atlantic.

prep.title: Buy the layers we do not own yet
prep.note: Waterproof AND windproof shells, wool base layers, and boots broken in before we go. New boots on day four is how a trip gets ruined.
